content:
I’m a Photoshop guy not a Dreamweaver guy, and I’m reaching a point where my lack of knowledge is starting to catch up to me. I have the entire plan and design of what I want to do, but I have no idea how to implement it, where I should even implement it in, and how feasible the ideas would be realistically. I’m currently using Obsidian Publish rerouted through a Cloudflare custom domain w/ JS injects, but I would like it to be completely self hosted(?) (I have no idea how to do this or what I’m talking about).
I would prefer to detail it more in DMs, but here are some of the major elements I’d love to be able to make:
- The entire base of the site looking like you’re navigating someone’s personal desktop. Bar at the bottom, icons that have hover over states, things needing to be double clicked, pop up windows that open in-site and can be dragged around, minimised or x’d. I managed to make a pop up window through a JS inject, but certain browsers thought it was an ad and blocked it, so I gave up.
- A fake email app that’s capable of receiving ‘new mail’ once a state in the website has been achieved. I assume this would be some kind of flag check to see if a certain action has been done? How would you make it so it doesn’t reset when they close the tab?
- Password protected apps or pages where you don’t have to re-enter it after you got it correct the first time
- Certain actions like introductory pop ups that will only appear when you open the site for the first time
- A ‘personality test’ style application where there are 16 possible results. The player would choose one out of many dialogue options in a series of questions, and at the end it’ll show you a graph of how much you scored in each of the 16 categories. I’m guessing that you would make each option worth a certain value and + or - them depending on the choices?
I would love to know if any of this sounds doable and how I would go about it, how achievable it would be for an idiot to make, etc. I would really like to work together with someone on this as every time I revisit it I get a headache in a new place. Thanks!

  submitted by   /u/egoraptorschins
[link]   [comments]
